protocol.name: wlr_output_power_management_unstable_v1

interface.name: zwlr_output_power_manager_v1

description.summary: manager to alter output power management

interface.description:
This interface allows a client to control power management modes of outputs that are currently part of the compositor space. The intent is to allow special clients like desktop shells to power down outputs when the system is idle.
To modify outputs not currently part of the compositor space see wlr-output-management.
Warning! The protocol described in this file is experimental and backward incompatible changes may be made. Backward compatible changes may be added together with the corresponding interface version bump. Backward incompatible changes are done by bumping the version number in the protocol and interface names and resetting the interface version. Once the protocol is to be declared stable, the 'z' prefix and the version number in the protocol and interface names are removed and the interface version number is reset.

request.name: get_output_power

description.summary: get a power management interface for an output

request.description: Create a output power management interface given an output.

request.name: destroy
request.description: All objects created by the manager will still remain valid, until their appropriate destroy request has been called.

interface.name: zwlr_output_power_v1
interface.description: This object offers requests to set the power management mode of an output.

entry.name: off
entry.summary: DPMS off: the output is turned off completely

entry.name: on
entry.summary: DPMS on: the output is turned on, displaying its normal contents

request.name: set_mode

description.summary: set an output's power management mode

request.description: Set an output's power management mode to the provided value. The changes take place immediately. If the compositor cannot apply the new power management mode for a specific output, the output power management object will emit the failed event.

request.name: destroy
request.description: Destroys the output power management object. This does not change the mode of the output.

event.name: mode

description.summary: report a power management mode change

event.description:
Report the power management mode change of an output.
The mode event is sent after an output changed its power management mode. The reason can be a client using set_mode or the compositor deciding to change an output's mode. This event is also sent immediately when the object is created so the client is informed about the current power management mode as soon as it can start issuing requests.

event.name: failed
event.description:
This event indicates that the output power management object is no longer valid. This can happen for a number of reasons, including: - The output disappeared. - The compositor cannot apply the power management mode requested by the client.
The client should clean up the resources associated with the output power management object in response to this event.
